Package net.apartium.cocoabeans.schematic.format

Class BlockChunkIndexEncoder

java.lang.Object
net.apartium.cocoabeans.schematic.format.BlockChunkIndexEncoder
All Implemented Interfaces:
IndexEncoder
@AvailableSince("0.0.46") public class BlockChunkIndexEncoder extends Object implements IndexEncoder
An IndexEncoder that organises block placements in a recursive quadtree-like chunk hierarchy and serialises that hierarchy to a compact binary index.

Binary format

   [1 byte]  layers   – tree depth; the spatial scale of the root is 4^layers
   [node…]  BFS-ordered chunk nodes (see below)
Each chunk node contains: 
   [8 bytes] mask     – 64-bit bitmask; bit i is set when child slot i is occupied
   [8 bytes] × popcount(mask)
             entries  – for leaf nodes: byte offset into the block-data stream;
                        for inner nodes: byte offset of the child chunk node

Inner-node offsets are back-patched after the child node is written, so the format supports forward references without a separate relocation table.

Spatial layout

Each chunk covers a 4×4×4 region of block positions (see BlockChunkImpl.SIZE). The three flat indices i0, i1, i2 are mapped to world-space axes via the supplied AxisOrder, allowing the encoder to be axis-agnostic.

See Also:
  • IndexEncoder
  • BlockChunkImpl

    • ID

      public static final int ID
      Numeric identifier that distinguishes this encoder in the schematic file header.

      public BlockChunkIndexEncoder()

  • Method Details

    • read

      public BlockIterator read(net.apartium.cocoabeans.seekable.SeekableInputStream indexIn, net.apartium.cocoabeans.space.axis.AxisOrder axisOrder, BlockDataEncoder blockDataEncoder, net.apartium.cocoabeans.seekable.SeekableInputStream blockIn) throws IOException
      Deserialises a block-chunk index from indexIn and returns an iterator over the block placements it describes.

      The method reads the tree depth from the first byte, computes the root scaler as 4^layers, and then recursively decodes every chunk node, resolving leaf offsets against blockIn via blockDataEncoder.

      Specified by:
      read in interface IndexEncoder
      Parameters:
      indexIn - seekable stream positioned at the start of the index section
      axisOrder - axis-to-index mapping used during spatial decoding
      blockDataEncoder - encoder used to deserialise individual BlockData values
      blockIn - seekable stream containing the raw block data
      Returns:
      an iterator over the decoded BlockPlacement values
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs while reading either stream

    • write

      public byte[] write(BlockIterator placements, net.apartium.cocoabeans.space.axis.AxisOrder axisOrder, Map<BlockData,Long> blockIndexes)
      Serialises the given block placements into the binary chunk-index format.

      All placements are first inserted into a MutableBlockChunk tree, which is then serialised layer by layer using a BFS traversal. The returned byte array can be embedded directly in a schematic file as the index section.

      Specified by:
      write in interface IndexEncoder
      Parameters:
      placements - iterator over the block placements to encode
      axisOrder - axis-to-index mapping used during tree construction
      blockIndexes - map from each BlockData to its byte offset in the block-data stream; every block referenced by placements must have an entry
      Returns:
      the serialised index as a byte array
      Throws:
      UncheckedIOException - if an I/O error occurs during serialisation
